response:
ISA, assume Walmart.com is developing a cross-border trade e-commerce marketplace integration platform. The platform aims to streamline the process of online selling by small merchants in one country and online buying of those products across international borders by international shoppers, enabling seamless integration of Walmart Ecommerce USA’s marketplace (walmart.com) with multiple of its Walmart International Online Marketplaces (e.g., walmart.uk, walmart.ca, walmart.mx) and also with the international marketplaces of Walmart’s Strategic Partners like JD.com in China and Rakuten.com in Japan. Top-tier U.K. companies already on the platform include STATSports, which has grown from its home base in Northern Ireland to be a global leader in wearable tech for professional and amateur sportspeople; Glasgow-based BuyBox, which sells home and garden products; Nodor, which manufactures and sells Red Dragon darts and other sporting equipment from its headquarters in Bridgend, Wales; and Leicester-based Pertemba, which exports a wide array of products to 18 countries worldwide.  

**Cross-Border Trade CBT E-commerce Marketplace Integration Platform Overview**:  
- *Purpose*: To develop a unified e-commerce platform that enables efficient B2B2C cross-border trade.  
- *Target Users*: Sellers and buyers in various international markets, including small businesses and large enterprises.  
- *Key Goals*: Enhance user experience, improve operational efficiency, and ensure compliance with international trade laws. The platform also includes Cross-border Fulfillment Services.  

Walmart Fulfillment Services (WFS) offers end-to-end fulfillment services for Walmart Marketplace sellers. With one of the world’s largest supply chains, Walmart is building on this to provide the scale, quality, cost, and efficiency cross-border online marketplace sellers need to grow a successful business on any of Walmart’s global marketplaces.  

**Fulfillment Model 1: Ship with Walmart Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace (Export Hub)**  
- FedEx will handle customs clearance and last-mile delivery.  
- Sellers can use their own domestic shipping or FedEx as the carrier.  
- The entire catalog must be shipped through only one Export Hub location.  
- Orders are placed in FedEx Cross Border after shipping.  
- Tracking numbers are updated with FedEx, providing end-to-end visibility.  

**Fulfillment Model 2: Fulfill with Walmart Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace**  
- Sellers store part of their catalog at FedEx Fulfillment Center (FC) in advance.  
- The location of a particular SKU is mutually exclusive,
